name: Batch Invoice Extractor – powered by easybits

nodes.name: Loop: One File at a Time

parameters.content:
## 📥 Upload Form
Hosts a web form where the user uploads one or more invoices (JPG, PNG, or PDF). Each submission starts the workflow.

parameters.formDescription:
Upload your PNG, JPG, or PDF files and we’ll automatically extract and structure the information for you.

Simply choose a file, submit it, and our system will process the content to generate usable data, saving you time on manual entry.

parameters.content:
## ✂️ Split Files
Turns the uploaded files into one item per file and renames each file's binary to `data` so the Extractor can read it. Keeps the original filename so we can show it in the summary later.

## 🔁 Loop One File at a Time
Feeds the Extractor a single file per pass. The Extractor bundles
everything it receives into one request and returns one result,
so looping is what gives us one clean result per invoice. When all files are done, the top output sends the summary to the form.

## 🧠 Extract Invoice Fields
Sends the file to the easybits Extractor, which returns the invoice fields under `data`. Set to continue on error, so a bad file gets flagged instead of stopping the whole batch.

## 🔗 Reattach Filename
The Extractor drops the filename when it returns its result. This combines the extracted fields with the original file info by position, so the filename rides along again.

## 🚦 Flag Status
Checks the core fields (invoice number, date, vendor, total). If any is missing, empty, whitespace, or "null", the invoice is marked ❌, otherwise ✅. Each result is stored so the summary can list them all
at the end.

## 📊 Save to Sheet
Adds one row per invoice to the Google Sheet with all extracted
fields. Value input is set to RAW so amounts and dates land exactly as extracted.

## 📝 Build Summary
Runs once after the loop finishes. Reads every stored result and builds the message the form shows, listing each file with its mark.

## ✅ Completion Screen
Shows the summary to the user after upload, so they can see which invoices processed cleanly and which need a second look.

## 📄 Batch Invoice Extractor (powered by easybits)

Upload one or more invoices (JPG, PNG, or PDF) through a web form.
Each file is read by the easybits Extractor, which pulls out the key
invoice fields and writes them to a Google Sheet. When the batch
finishes, the form shows a summary listing each file with a ✅ or ❌,
so you know at a glance whether anything needs a second look.

## 🔁 How it flows
Upload → split into one item per file → loop one file at a time →
extract fields → reattach the filename → check the core fields →
append a row → show the summary.

## ❓ Why one file at a time
The Extractor bundles every file it receives into a single request
and returns one result. Feeding it a single file per loop pass is
what gives one clean result per invoice.

## ⚙️ Setup

**1. Install the Extractor node**
n8n Cloud: open the nodes panel and search "easybits". It is a
verified node, so it shows up directly. Add it.
Self-hosted: Settings → Community Nodes → Install, then enter
`@easybits/n8n-nodes-extractor`.

**2. Create your pipeline**
Sign up at https://extractor.easybits.tech/, create a
pipeline, and add your fields (invoice_number, invoice_date, and so
on), giving each a short description of what to pull. Or skip the
manual setup: upload an example invoice and use the auto-mapping
feature to detect and map all the fields for you, then tweak the
descriptions as needed. The free tier covers 50 extractions per
month. Copy the API key when done.

**3. Connect it in n8n**
On the Extract node, create an easybits credential and paste the API
key. Set the input to read the binary property named `data`, which
is what the Split node produces.

**4. Point the Sheet node at your own Google Sheet**
Pick your spreadsheet and tab, and set value input to RAW so amounts
and dates are stored exactly as extracted.

Docs: extractor.easybits.tech/documentation/integration
